summary: CostDrift promotes a bare YYYY-MM-DD --since to end-of-day before comparing against RFC3339 snapshot timestamps so same-day snapshots are included in the baseline window.
reason: Greptile review (PR #576): s.at is stored as RFC3339 ("2026-04-01T08:00:00Z") but --since is documented as YYYY-MM-DD. Lexicographically "2026-04-01T08:00:00Z" sorts after "2026-04-01", so s.at <= since was false for every same-day snapshot — the baseline silently slipped to the previous day.
validated_outcome: go build, go vet, go test ./internal/pricebook/ all clean
